adolescent_relevance: Adolescent windows are tracked because developmental biology, social context, and exposure intensity can shift long-term trajectories.
age_of_onset_distribution: Adolescence to late adulthood depending on subtype and context.
ancestry_specific_risks: Effect sizes can shift with ancestry representation and exposure distribution differences.
epigenetic_factors: DNA methylation and chromatin-state changes in disease-linked tissues.

evidence_table.label: Integrated genomic and exposure signal for Breast Cancer

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Breast Cancer burden likely increases when endocrine disruptors combines with elevated inherited susceptibility.

gene_environment_interactions: Additive model includes genotype, exposure, and interaction terms.
Multiplicative and ancestry-aware sensitivity analyses are supported in the methods layer.

heritability_estimate.source: GENARCH synthesis of GWAS Catalog and major epidemiologic cohorts

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS interpretation remains population-level and educational with ancestry-aware limitations.

top_loci.ancestry_composition: Mixed ancestry cohorts with uneven but expanding representation.
top_loci.replication_status: Replicated in independent cohorts with context-dependent heterogeneity.

genetic_architecture_type: Polygenic with monogenic enrichment in selected subgroups.
inheritance_pattern: Polygenic or mixed architecture depending on subtype and penetrance profile.
metabolomic_markers: Lipid, oxidative stress, and energy metabolism signatures.
name: Breast Cancer
pharmacogenomic_markers: Disease-specific pharmacogenomic marker evidence is curated where available.

population_equity.data_gaps: Underrepresented populations and low-resource settings remain incompletely characterized.
population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Evidence includes multi-ancestry data but representation remains uneven by region.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Cross-ancestry transferability can vary because LD structure and exposure context differ.

prevalence_global: Global burden with region-specific incidence and exposure gradients.
primary_pathophysiology: Breast Cancer reflects multi-layer biologic dysregulation linking molecular signaling, tissue response, and contextual exposures.
proteomic_markers: Cytokine and tissue injury proteins with disease-context relevance.
rare_high_penetrance_variants: Rare high-impact coding variants in BRCA1

references.title: GENARCH curated synthesis for Breast Cancer

sex_bias: Sex bias varies by disease subtype and life-course stage.
summary: Breast Cancer is represented in GENARCH as a population-level condition where inherited architecture, pathway biology, and environmental burden jointly shape observed epidemiologic variation. This atlas entry is educational and does not provide individualized diagnosis or medical advice.
transcriptomic_signatures: Inflammatory and stress-response transcript modules associated with burden.
validated_prs_studies: Breast Cancer PRS has cohort-level validation in curated studies.
